CanaryArrType.description: Arr family a rollout is scoped to. A rollout never spans types.

CanaryRolloutStatus.description:
Live state machine of a staged rollout:
- `canary_running`: canary sync is executing inline
- `awaiting_confirmation`: canary passed, waiting at the verification gate
- `rolling_out`: batched rollout job is syncing remaining instances
- `completed`: all remaining instances synced cleanly
- `aborted`: gate declined, or canary failed/partial-abort/skipped (remaining untouched)
- `failed`: rollout ran but one or more remaining instances failed

CanaryOutcomeStatus.description: Classified terminal status of the canary sync run.

CanaryPartialPolicy.description:
How a `partial` canary outcome is treated:
- `gate`: pass-with-warning; continue to the verification gate
- `abort`: treat as failure; remaining instances are never touched

CanaryJobRunStatus.description: Per-instance outcome status following JobRunStatus semantics.

instanceId.description: Arr instance ID selected as a rollout target.

instanceName.description: Denormalized instance name captured at rollout start.

CanarySyncRunResult.description: Verbatim executeSyncJob return, surfaced when a start auto-skips to a plain sync.

canaryInstanceId.description: Explicit canary instance. Highest selection precedence.

sections.description: Optional section filters. Omit for all configured sections.

maxBatchSize.description: Max remaining instances synced per batch. Falls back to the settings default.

CanaryGeneratePreviewResult.description:
Read-only per-instance preview payload produced by the Canary coordinator. This matches the
runtime `GeneratePreviewResult` contract; it is not a stored Sync Preview and therefore has no
preview id, expiry timestamp, or top-level failure field.

instanceName.description: Arr instance name at preview-generation time.

createdAtMs.description: Preview generation time as Unix epoch milliseconds.

sectionOutcomes.description: Per-section evidence; any failure makes aggregate Canary evidence unavailable.

version.description: Persisted Canary remaining-preview evidence schema version.

generatedAt.description: Time the complete exact-target evidence snapshot was generated.

previews.description:
Complete previews for the persisted remaining target set. An empty mutation summary is a
valid no-changes result and is distinct from unavailable evidence.

version.description: Persisted Canary remaining-preview evidence schema version.

generatedAt.description: Time this unavailable evidence result was recorded.

partialPreviews.description:
Successfully generated diagnostic pieces, if any. Partial previews never authorize
promotion and do not represent complete target coverage.

CanaryRemainingPreviewEvidence.description:
Durable, versioned evidence for the exact persisted remaining-target cohort. Availability is
explicit and must never be inferred from preview-array length. Null, legacy, corrupt, partial,
cross-Arr, or target-mismatched stored data is exposed as unavailable and cannot authorize
Proceed.

canaryInstanceId.description: Null when the canary instance was deleted after this run.

canaryStatus.description: Null while the canary is still running.

canarySyncHistoryId.description: FK to the canary's sync_history row (diagnostics source).

sections.description: Requested sections, or null for all configured sections.

batchCursor.description: Index into remainingTargets the rollout job has reached (resumable).

stateToken.description: Value-guard token for /proceed and /abort; re-issued on every transition.

remainingCount.description: Number of remaining targets, derived from remainingTargets length.

completedCount.description: Number of instances rolled out, derived from rolloutResults length.

CanaryStartSkipped.description: Returned when a single eligible same-arr_type target auto-skips the staged flow.

CanaryStartGated.description:
Returned when the canary passed and the rollout is awaiting confirmation. The rollout carries
the persisted remaining-preview evidence used by detail reads and promotion authorization.

CanaryStartResult.description:
Two-headed start response distinguished by the boolean `skipped` constant:
- `skipped: true`: single-target auto-skip ran a plain sync
- `skipped: false`: staged rollout persisted and awaiting confirmation

enabled.description: Opt-in. When false the staged flow is hidden and start falls through to a plain sync.

autoSelect.description: Allow least-critical auto-pick when no explicit or default canary is set.

stateToken.description: The state_token the client was shown; value-guards concurrent/double-proceed.
The state_token the client was shown; value-guards concurrent proceed/abort.

GoalCategory.description: Closed set of semantic categories a custom format is classified into.

GoalPlan.description: Deterministic translation of a goal into concrete scores + thresholds.

GoalQualityLadderItem.description: One row of the ceiling-derived quality ladder (issue #221).

mapped.description: False when the row is not present/derivable for the target arr (left unchanged).

GoalQualityLadder.description: Ceiling-derived quality-ladder configuration (issue #221), always shown before persistence. A per-arr ceiling reshapes the shared quality_profile_qualities row set for every Arr that syncs the profile; sharedLadderNote surfaces that when the profile is sibling-compatible.

cutoff.description: PCD quality name carrying the cutoff; null when no ladder change is derivable.

GoalBinding.description: Persisted intent metadata for a goal-governed quality profile.

appliedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the last apply

GoalPreviewResponse.description: Non-persisting preview — the plan plus the authoritative sandbox config diff.

expectedEngineVersion.description: The engine version the client computed against; a mismatch yields 409.

configDiff.description: Authoritative sandbox config diff of the persisted ladder + scoring, captured before persist so it matches the preview diff for the same request (issue #221).

applyId.description: The apply-journal row id for this apply (issue #236).

applyStatus.description: The latest apply-journal outcome for this profile target, or null if none has been attempted. Surfaces a failed/pending apply — including a failure that never wrote a binding (issue #236).

GoalRecoveryAction.description: The safe operator recovery action for a failed or pending Quality Goals apply (issue #236).

GoalApplyStatus.description: Durable outcome of a Quality Goals apply/reconcile attempt (issue #236). Reports whether scoring changed, the binding's terminal state, and the safe recovery action, so a partial write is never unreported.

scoringChanged.description: Whether the scoring ops reached (or may have reached) their intended terminal state.

GoalApplyFailure.description: Structured failure body for a Quality Goals apply/reconcile (issue #236). `message` mirrors ErrorResponse's human text (the UI failure reader falls back to `error`), and `applyStatus` carries the reported outcome + recovery action.

GoalReconcileRequest.description: Recover a partial or pending Quality Goals apply by re-driving the recorded intent (#236).

reconciled.description: Whether ops were persisted this call (false when live already matched the intent).

alreadyApplied.description: Whether the live state already equalled the recorded intent (a no-op reconcile).

TrashGuideSyncFailureCode.description: Closed, safe vocabulary of TRaSH sync failure reasons. Assigned by outcome/error type only — never by message parsing — so no raw diagnostic can leak.

TrashGuideSyncFailureReason.description: Typed, closed, SAFE failure evidence. message/recoveryAction are pre-authored copy that never embeds raw exception text, git/parser diagnostics, credentials, or hostnames.

TrashGuideSyncCounts.description: Fetched/applied counts for a TRaSH sync run.

TrashGuideSyncRunEvidence.description: Versioned, structured terminal evidence for one TRaSH sync run, serialized into job_run_history.output. Survives a source hard-delete via its embedded identity snapshot.

TrashGuideSyncStatusView.description: Single wire view of a source's current queue slot + latest terminal run, reused by the POST response and the GET status resolver.

TrashGuideSyncQueuedResponse.description: Manual-sync enqueue acknowledgement, linking to exactly one current-or-terminal run.

TrashGuideSyncDedupeResponse.description: Already-running dedupe response — links to the existing in-flight run rather than acking a new one.

EntityType.description:
Portable PCD entity identifiers used by import/export endpoints.

Lidarr media-management entities are first-class portable contract members:
- `lidarr_naming`
- `lidarr_media_settings`
- `lidarr_quality_definitions`

Runtime validation is deterministic and fail-fast for Lidarr payloads:
payloads are rejected when required fields are missing, when unsupported
fields are present, or when cross-family fields are mixed into the payload
(for example Radarr naming fields with `lidarr_naming`).

Clients must emit `lidarr_*` entity types for Lidarr entities.

format.description:
Migration payload format marker (`json` for JSON payloads, `yaml` for YAML
in future hybrid ingestion flows).

version.description: Migration schema contract version for hybrid payloads.

PortableMigrationMetadata.description:
Optional metadata for hybrid JSON/YAML migration envelopes.
Existing payloads are backward-compatible when omitted.

data.description:
The portable entity payload. Shape varies by entityType:
- `delay_profile` → PortableDelayProfile
- `regular_expression` → PortableRegularExpression
- `custom_format` → PortableCustomFormat
- `quality_profile` → PortableQualityProfile (contains PortableCustomFormatScore with family-scoped arrType)
- `radarr_naming` → PortableRadarrNaming
- `sonarr_naming` → PortableSonarrNaming
- `lidarr_naming` → PortableLidarrNaming
- `radarr_media_settings` → PortableMediaSettings
- `sonarr_media_settings` → PortableMediaSettings
- `lidarr_media_settings` → PortableLidarrMediaSettings
- `radarr_quality_definitions` → PortableQualityDefinitions
- `sonarr_quality_definitions` → PortableQualityDefinitions
- `lidarr_metadata_profile` → PortableLidarrMetadataProfile
- `lidarr_quality_definitions` → PortableLidarrQualityDefinitions

`lidarr_*` payloads are validated against strict allowlists so mixed
cross-family payloads fail fast during import.

databaseId.description: The PCD database ID to import into

layer.description: The operation layer to write to

data.description:
The portable entity payload (same shape as ExportResponse.data).
For `lidarr_*` entity types, runtime validation rejects mixed
cross-family payload fields and unknown fields.

items.description: Condition data (shape varies by condition type)

arrType.description:
Arr target for this score mapping. `lidarr` is a first-class value
so quality profile custom-format scores can be scoped to a specific Arr
app family. `all` applies the score to every supported family.

Non-regression contract: existing `radarr`, `sonarr`, and `all`
enum semantics are unchanged; `lidarr` is an explicit additional arr
target value and does not introduce fallback alias behavior.

PortableSonarrNaming.description: Portable naming payload for Sonarr entities.

PortableLidarrNaming.description:
Portable naming payload for `lidarr_naming`.
The wire shape matches `PortableSonarrNaming` (shared column structure).
Field semantics are Lidarr-native (artist/album/track tokens).

PortableMediaSettings.description: Shared media-management settings payload shape used across Arr families.

PortableLidarrMediaSettings.description:
Portable payload for `lidarr_media_settings`.
The shape matches `PortableMediaSettings`.

PortableQualityDefinitions.description: Shared quality-definition payload shape used across Arr families.

items.description: Quality definition entry with min/max/preferred sizes

PortableLidarrQualityDefinitions.description:
Portable payload for `lidarr_quality_definitions`.
The shape matches `PortableQualityDefinitions`.

SnapshotType.description: Whether the snapshot was auto-created or user-initiated

SnapshotTrigger.description:
What event triggered the snapshot creation.
- pull: before a PCD repository pull/refresh
- sync: before an Arr instance sync
- manual: user-initiated via API
- rollback: system pre-rollback auto-capture taken before a restore rewinds the op log

opsSequenceMaxId.description: Maximum pcd_ops ID at snapshot time

targetInstanceIds.description: Arr instance IDs targeted by this operation

opsWrittenSince.description: Number of ops written after this snapshot (count of pcd_ops rows with id greater than opsSequenceMaxId)

isRestorable.description:
Whether this snapshot can be safely restored. True only when the snapshot's
published-op set reconstructs and its recomputed fingerprint matches the stored
cacheStateHash (fail-closed). Legacy snapshots without a fingerprint are false.

total.description: Total count matching the query filters

CreateManualSnapshotRequest.description: Request body for creating a manual snapshot. All fields are optional.

RollbackSection.description:
A group of entity changes for one resolved-config family. Reuses the sync-preview
EntityChange/FieldChange shapes. Diff direction is PCD-to-PCD: each FieldChange.current
is the CURRENT PCD desired-state and FieldChange.desired is the SNAPSHOT restore-target
state (NOT live Arr state).

entityType.description: Namespaced entity type; per-arr families are "${entityType}:${arrType}"

RollbackPreview.description:
Mandatory PCD-to-PCD preview of what restoring a snapshot would change in the PCD desired
state. Never contacts an Arr instance. When reconstructable is false the snapshot cannot
be safely restored and sections is empty.

reconstructable.description: Whether the snapshot verifies against its fingerprint and can be restored

reason.description: When not reconstructable, why (legacy/no-fingerprint or diverged history)

currentStateHash.description: Live published-op fingerprint at preview time; echo as expectedCurrentStateHash on execute

ExecuteRollbackRequest.description:
Request body to execute a rollback. expectedCurrentStateHash is a from-state value-guard
echoed from the preview's currentStateHash; if the live state changed since the preview
the request is rejected with 422.

expectedCurrentStateHash.description: The preview's currentStateHash (may be empty string when no published ops exist)

opsUndone.description: Ops written after the snapshot that were superseded

opsReactivated.description: Pre-snapshot ops deactivated since that were re-published

preRollbackSnapshotId.description: The durable pre-rollback capture that makes this rollback reversible

completed.description: Whether the wizard has been marked completed

dismissedAt.description: Timestamp the wizard was dismissed, or null if never dismissed

hasArrInstance.description: Whether at least one Arr instance has been connected

hasDatabase.description: Whether at least one PCD database has been linked

hasProfileSelections.description: Whether at least one instance has quality profile selections synced

prerequisites.description: Setup prerequisite progress derived from real entity state

configured.description: Whether PRAXRR_DEFAULT_DB_URL resolves to a non-empty value

url.description: Resolved default database URL, or null when not configured

alreadyLinked.description: Whether any PCD database is already linked

defaultDatabase.description: Default database auto-link info used to render the link-database step

SetupStatePatchRequest.description: Advance the wizard to the given step

appName.description: Arr application name, present on success

version.description: Arr application version, present on success

reason.description: Sanitized failure reason, present on failure

scope.description: Parity entity or workflow surface this semantic difference applies to

apps.description: Arr apps this entry applies to

summary.description: One-line summary of the semantic divergence

detail.description: Full explanation of why the divergence exists

suggestion.description: Suggested alternative or mitigation, when applicable

sourceRefs.description: Prose file/symbol anchors for drift audits, not imports

compatibleArrTypes.description: Arr app types this profile's enabled qualities are compatible with

basis.description: Algorithm basis used to compute this compatibility verdict

entities.description: PCD config entities tracked by the Cross-Arr Parity Map

apps.description: Arr app types represented as matrix columns

label.description: Human-readable label for this entity row

radarr.description: Radarr support status for this entity

sonarr.description: Sonarr support status for this entity

lidarr.description: Lidarr support status for this entity

matrix.description: One row per parity entity, with a per-app support status column

profiles.description: Per-profile Arr-type compatibility, when quality profile data is available

ArrSupportTier.description: Coarse support tier for a detected (arr_type, application version) pair

minimumRecommended.description: Versions below this are degraded (below recommended)

latestTested.description: Latest tested version; newer versions stay supported with an informational warning

eolBelow.description: Versions at or below this are degraded with an end-of-support advisory

breakingAtOrAbove.description: Versions at or above this are unsupported (known or expected breaking major)

degradedFeatures.description: Feature surfaces gated to degraded when the tier is degraded

eolNote.description: Extra advisory copy surfaced with an end-of-support warning

feature.description: Feature surface (workflow or sync) this row describes

tiers.description: Per-tier resolved availability status for this feature

features.description: Per-feature availability across every support tier

ResolvedLayer.description:
PCD configuration layer represented by a resolved-state response:
- `base`: schema + base + tweaks ops only (user ops omitted)
- `user`: user-op overrides relative to the base layer, expressed as a
  field-level diff
- `resolved`: the fully replayed state (schema -> base -> tweaks -> user),
  matching what sync applies to Arr instances

FieldLineage.description:
Exact provenance for one resolved nested field. `status` is the gate:
`resolved` rows name a source; `ambiguous`/`unavailable` rows make no source
claim. `schema-default` means no op explicitly wrote the column and its value
equals the parsed schema DEFAULT; it is never inferred from the mere absence
of a user override.

fieldPath.description:
Nested field path, byte-identical to the diff convention used by
`overrides` (e.g. `conditions["HDR"].negate`,
`orderedItems["WEB"].members[0].name`).

status.description:
`resolved`: a source is named. `ambiguous`: evidence conflicts, is
pending, or the op SQL was unparseable. `unavailable`: no establishing
op and no default backs this path.

sourceLayer.description: The establishing layer; null unless `status` is `resolved`.

sourceKind.description: Source classification, aligned with `sourceLayer` for resolved rows.

opId.description:
Establishing `pcd_ops` id for base/user (DB) ops; null for file layers
(schema/tweaks) and schema-default.

opRef.description:
File-layer op identity (schema/tweaks), which has no `pcd_ops` row; null
for DB ops.

explicit.description: True when an op explicitly named this column (distinct from an implicit default).

valueEqualsDefault.description:
Display-only signal indicating the resolved value equals the schema
default. Never used to classify provenance. Absent when not comparable.

present.description: Whether the entity exists in the requested layer

entity.description:
Canonical Portable payload for the entity in the requested layer.
Present for `layer=base` and `layer=resolved` when `present` is true;
null when the entity is absent from that layer or when `layer=user`.
Shape varies by entityType (and arrType for per-arr-app entity
types):
- `delayProfile` -> PortableDelayProfile
- `regularExpression` -> PortableRegularExpression
- `customFormat` -> PortableCustomFormat
- `qualityProfile` -> PortableQualityProfile
- `naming` (radarr) -> PortableRadarrNaming
- `naming` (sonarr) -> PortableSonarrNaming
- `naming` (lidarr) -> PortableLidarrNaming
- `mediaSettings` (radarr, sonarr) -> PortableMediaSettings
- `mediaSettings` (lidarr) -> PortableLidarrMediaSettings
- `qualityDefinitions` (radarr, sonarr) -> PortableQualityDefinitions
- `qualityDefinitions` (lidarr) -> PortableLidarrQualityDefinitions
- `lidarrMetadataProfile` -> PortableLidarrMetadataProfile

overrides.description:
Field-level diff of user-op overrides relative to the base layer.
Present only for `layer=user`; an empty array means the resolved
value matches base exactly.

hasPendingConflict.description:
True when a pending value-guard conflict targets this entity (see
`pcd_op_history`). The resolved value must not be treated as
unambiguous while this is true.

lineage.description:
Exact per-field lineage. Present only when `includeLineage=true` and
`layer=resolved`; null/absent otherwise.

lineageStatus.description:
Entity-level lineage rollup. `ambiguous` when a pending value-guard
conflict withholds all field claims; `unavailable` when the entity is
absent. Present only alongside `lineage`.

entities.description: Per-entity resolved state for the requested layer

arrType.description:
Arr instance family, or null when the instance's own `arr_type` value is
unrecognized (the `incompatible` error case).

compatible.description: Whether this entity type is supported for the instance's arr_type

present.description: Whether the entity is present in this instance's desired payload

desired.description:
Per-instance transformed-desired Portable payload, present when
`compatible` and `present` are both true. Uses the same
entityType/arrType-to-Portable-shape mapping as
`ResolvedEntityState.entity`.

actual.description:
Raw live Arr state for this instance, only populated when
`includeLive=true` and the live fetch succeeded. Shape is the
upstream Arr API payload, not a Portable shape.

error.description:
Sanitized reason when this instance's desired/live lookup failed.
Raw error detail never appears here; full detail is logged
server-side only. Superset of the single-instance live-diff reason
enum: `incompatible` (unrecognized arr_type), `rate-limited`
(per-instance live fetch throttled), and `error` (unexpected
failure, detail logged not surfaced) are comparison-specific.
`not_configured` means the section has no sync configuration on the
instance at all.

instanceId.description: Arr instance ID this diff row belongs to

changes.description:
Field-level changes relative to the first compatible
instance's desired payload.

diffs.description:
Pairwise diffs of each compatible instance's desired payload against
the first compatible instance (the baseline).

instanceId.description: Arr instance ID the live diff was computed against

changes.description: Desired-vs-actual field changes; an empty array means the entity is in sync

GraphNodeKind.description:
PCD entity family a dependency-graph node represents. `quality` and
`quality_definition` are leaf kinds with no per-id editor route (see
`GraphNode.routeId`).

GraphArrType.description:
Arr scope of a dependency edge. `all` is a first-class, distinct value (an
edge that applies to every Arr app); it is never expanded into per-arr
edges and never establishes per-arr quality-profile compatibility.

GraphEdgeKind.description:
Relationship family. Direction is always referrer (`from`) -> dependency
(`to`).

name.description: Name-keyed identifier of the referenced entity

routeId.description:
Integer editor id for linkable kinds (`custom_format`,
`regular_expression`, `quality_profile`); null for leaf kinds
(`quality`, `quality_definition`) that have no per-id editor route.

inDegree.description: Number of edges pointing at this node (how many entities reference it)

outDegree.description: Number of edges leaving this node (how many entities it depends on)

compatibleArrTypes.description:
Arr apps this node is valid for, present only for `quality_profile` and
`quality` nodes. For quality profiles it is derived from
`computeProfileCompatibility` (never from `arr_type='all'` scores).

score.description: Custom-format score, present only on `quality_profile_custom_format` edges

groupName.description: Quality group name, present only on grouped `quality_profile_quality` edges

nodes.description: Every in-scope entity node, with in/out degree and (where applicable) compatibility

edges.description: Referrer -> dependency edges, filtered by the requested arrType/nodeKind

arrTypesPresent.description: Distinct arr scopes present across the returned edges

truncated.description: True when the graph exceeded the server-side edge cap and was truncated

direction.description:
`dependents` (default) = entities that reference this node (reverse
traversal); `dependencies` = entities this node references (forward).

edges.description: All edges discovered by the bounded traversal

byArrType.description: The same edges grouped by arrType (arr scopes never collapsed)

counts.description: Count of distinct related entities keyed by node kind, plus `total`

hasDownstream.description: True when at least one edge was found (there is downstream impact)

truncated.description: True when the traversal hit its depth or visited cap and stopped early

ConfigHealthBand.description: Health band derived from the 0–100 rollup. `unknown` means every enabled criterion was skipped.

ConfigHealthTone.description: Presentation hint for a suggestion; health tone never exceeds `warning`.

ConfigHealthSuggestion.description: A non-judgmental remediation line rendered through the shared narration surface.

ConfigHealthCriterion.description: One criterion's contribution to a scored unit.

id.description: Criterion id — one of completeness, drift, coherence, compatibility, trash_alignment.

score.description: 0–100 sub-score, or null when the criterion could not be evaluated (excluded from the rollup).

ConfigHealthScoredUnit.description: A scored unit (whole instance or one profile) — the rollup number, its band, and the breakdown.

averageScore.description: Mean overall score across scored instances, or null when none could be scored.

ConfigHealthTrendPointState.description:
Availability of the point's selected scope. `unknown` is persisted scoring without a measured
score, `profile-missing` preserves a snapshot where the exact profile was absent, and
`not-recorded` preserves snapshot identity when stored evidence is unusable.

ConfigHealthTrendCriterionState.description: Availability of one persisted overall criterion observation.

ConfigHealthTrendRetention.description:
Current global retention policy and observed availability for this instance. These values do not
identify why any older point is absent or prove which retention rule removed it.

missing.description: Points whose selected evidence is profile-missing or not-recorded.

ConfigHealthTrendEngineBoundary.description: Start of one contiguous persisted engine-version run in canonical point order.

ConfigHealthTrendCriterion.description:
One persisted overall criterion observation. Profile-scoped points do not expose criteria.
Nullable numeric fields remain distinct from measured zero.

ConfigHealthTrendPoint.description: One persisted snapshot observation in `generatedAt ASC, snapshotId ASC` order.

criteria.description: Persisted overall criterion observations; empty for exact-profile scope.

ConfigHealthTrendsResponse.description:
Canonical bounded historical result shared by chart/table JSON and JSON/CSV export selection.
Empty selections are successful and contain `points: []` with the same metadata shape.

availableProfiles.description: Exact historical profile names in deterministic order.

ConfigHealthSettingsUpdateRequest.description: Partial settings update. `expectedEngineVersion` guards against a stale client (409 on mismatch).

SecurityShieldBand.description: Shield band derived from the 0–100 rollup. `unknown` only when every check was skipped.

SecurityCheckStatus.description: Presentation status derived from the check's score and severity.

SecurityTransportTier.description: How an Arr connection's target host is classified from Praxrr's own knowledge (no probing).

SecurityDnsOutcome.description: Closed result of bounded DNS evidence gathering for one stored Arr hostname.

SecurityDnsEvidenceSource.description: Provenance of a DNS observation; cache hits retain the original observation time.

SecurityDnsAddressClassCounts.description: Bounded address-class counts for one DNS record family; raw addresses are never exposed.

SecurityDnsTransportEvidence.description: Safe aggregate DNS evidence; excludes addresses, resolver details, CNAMEs, and error text.

observedAt.description: Original observation time, or null when no DNS observation exists.

SecurityFix.description: A concrete remediation target. `kind` discriminates which optional fields are present.

href.description: Present for settings-link, instance-link, and docs.

SecurityRecommendation.description: A non-shaming remediation line paired with its concrete fix.

SecurityCheck.description: One scored check's contribution to the shield report.

score.description: 0–100 sub-score, or null when the check could not be evaluated (excluded from the rollup).

SecurityTransportRow.description: One enabled Arr connection's transport posture (host only — never the API key).

SecurityAssurance.description: An always-on protection surfaced as a verified affirmation (contributes zero to the score).

SecurityAdvisory.description: A real posture note whose exploitability Praxrr cannot observe, so it informs without a score.

SecurityTopAction.description: A ranked "to reach Hardened" step derived from a scored check below 100.

SecurityPostureSummaryResponse.description: The live shield report for one deployment — computed on demand, never persisted.

bandCappedBy.description: The critical finding that lowered the band below the numeric average, or null.

TimelineSource.description: The event source a timeline event originates from.

TimelineScopeKind.description: Which instance axis an event is scoped to.

TimelineStatus.description: Normalized, cross-source status used for filtering and badge colour.

TimelineBadge.description: Badge variant used to colour the event row.

TimelineScope.description: Where an event happened. `id` is null when the Arr instance was deleted; `label` is retained.

TimelineAnnotation.description: A user note attached to a single timeline event.

TimelineEvent.description: A single normalized timeline event with inline annotations.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC event time (normalized across source dialects).

metrics.description: Small source-specific bag of counts sufficient to render the row.

detailHref.description: Deep-link into the owning feature's existing detail surface.

TimelineSourceCounts.description: Per-source event counts over the same filtered/gated set.

WebAuthnRegistrationOptionsResponse.description: WebAuthn credential creation options (PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptionsJSON), passed verbatim to the browser's navigator.credentials.create(). The inner shape is owned by @simplewebauthn and treated as opaque here.

response.description: RegistrationResponseJSON returned by the browser startRegistration().

name.description: Optional friendly credential name. Defaults to a user-agent-derived name when omitted.

WebAuthnCredentialSummary.description: Client-safe view of a registered passkey (no key material).

WebAuthnAuthenticationOptionsResponse.description: WebAuthn assertion options (PublicKeyCredentialRequestOptionsJSON) for discoverable/passwordless login, passed verbatim to navigator.credentials.get().

response.description: AuthenticationResponseJSON returned by the browser startAuthentication().
